question: What is the process for amending the Constitution of Zimbabwe?
ground_truth: Section 328 provides that the Constitution may be amended by a bill passed by at least two-thirds of the members of Parliament and approved by at least two-thirds of the members of the Senate.
contexts: Section 328 of the Constitution provides that the Constitution may be amended by a bill passed by at least two-thirds of the members of Parliament and approved by at least two-thirds of the members of the Senate.

question: What political rights are guaranteed to Zimbabwean citizens under section 67?
ground_truth: Section 67 guarantees citizens the right to free, fair and regular elections, to make political choices freely, to form and join political parties, to campaign freely and peacefully, to participate in peaceful political activity, and every citizen who is eighteen or older has the right to vote and stand for public office.
contexts: Every Zimbabwean citizen has the right to free, fair and regular elections and to make political choices freely, to form, to join and to participate in the activities of a political party, to campaign freely and peacefully for a political party or cause, to participate in peaceful political activity, and every citizen who is of or over eighteen years of age has the right to vote in all elections and referendums, and to do so in secret, and to stand for election for public office and, if elected, to hold such office.

question: What does section 56 say about equality and non-discrimination?
ground_truth: Section 56 states that all persons are equal before the law and have the right to equal protection and benefit of the law. Women and men have the right to equal treatment and equal opportunities. Every person has the right not to be unfairly discriminated against on grounds such as nationality, race, tribe, place of birth, ethnic or social origin, language, religion, political affiliation, sex, gender, marital status, age, pregnancy, disability or economic or social status.
contexts: All persons are equal before the law and have the right to equal protection and benefit of the law. Women and men have the right to equal treatment, including the right to equal opportunities in political, economic, cultural and social spheres. Every person has the right not to be treated in an unfairly discriminatory manner on such grounds as their nationality, race, colour, tribe, place of birth, ethnic or social origin, language, class, religious belief, political affiliation, opinion, custom, culture, sex, gender, marital status, age, pregnancy, disability or economic or social status.

question: What rights does an arrested person have under section 50?
ground_truth: Section 50 gives arrested persons the right to be informed promptly of the reason for arrest, to contact a spouse, relative or legal practitioner without delay, to consult private legal and medical practitioners, to be treated humanely and with respect for dignity, to be released unless there are compelling reasons for detention, and to be brought before a court within forty-eight hours if not released.
contexts: Any person who is arrested must be informed at the time of arrest of the reason for the arrest; must be permitted, without delay, to contact their spouse or partner, a relative or legal practitioner, or anyone else of their choice; must be treated humanely and with respect for their inherent dignity; must be released unconditionally or on reasonable conditions unless there are compelling reasons justifying continued detention, and any person not released must be brought before a court as soon as possible and not later than forty-eight hours after the arrest.

question: Which languages are officially recognised in Zimbabwe under section 6?
ground_truth: Section 6 recognises Chewa, Chibarwe, English, Kalanga, Koisan, Nambya, Ndau, Ndebele, Shangani, Shona, sign language, Sotho, Tonga, Tswana, Venda and Xhosa as the officially recognised languages of Zimbabwe.
contexts: The following languages, namely Chewa, Chibarwe, English, Kalanga, Koisan, Nambya, Ndau, Ndebele, Shangani, Shona, sign language, Sotho, Tonga, Tswana, Venda and Xhosa, are the officially recognised languages of Zimbabwe.

question: What protections does section 61 provide regarding freedom of expression?
ground_truth: Section 61 protects the right to freedom of expression including seeking, receiving and communicating ideas and information, artistic expression, scientific research, creativity and academic freedom. It also protects freedom of the media. However, these freedoms exclude incitement to violence, advocacy of hatred or hate speech, malicious injury to reputation or dignity, and malicious or unwarranted breaches of privacy.
contexts: Every person has the right to freedom of expression, which includes freedom to seek, receive and communicate ideas and other information; freedom of artistic expression and scientific research and creativity; and academic freedom. Every person is entitled to freedom of the media. Freedom of expression and freedom of the media exclude incitement to violence, advocacy of hatred or hate speech, malicious injury to a person's reputation or dignity, or malicious or unwarranted breach of a person's right to privacy.

question: What labour rights are protected under section 65?
ground_truth: Section 65 protects the right to fair and safe labour practices, a fair and reasonable wage, the right to form and join trade unions, participate in lawful union activities, engage in collective job action including strike rights, just and satisfactory conditions of work, collective bargaining, equal remuneration for similar work, and fully paid maternity leave for women employees of at least three months.
contexts: Every person has the right to fair and safe labour practices and standards and to be paid a fair and reasonable wage. Every person has the right to form and join trade unions and every employee has the right to participate in collective job action, including the right to strike. Every employee is entitled to just, equitable and satisfactory conditions of work. Women and men have a right to equal remuneration for similar work. Women employees have a right to fully paid maternity leave for a period of at least three months.

question: What does section 27 require regarding education?
ground_truth: Section 27 requires the State to take all practical measures to promote free and compulsory basic education for children and higher and tertiary education. It also requires the State to ensure that girls are afforded the same opportunities as boys to obtain education at all levels.
contexts: The State must take all practical measures to promote free and compulsory basic education for children and higher and tertiary education. The State must take measures to ensure that girls are afforded the same opportunities as boys to obtain education at all levels.

question: Who is considered a Zimbabwean citizen by birth under section 36?
ground_truth: Section 36 states that a person is a Zimbabwean citizen by birth if they were born in Zimbabwe and either parent was a Zimbabwean citizen or any grandparent was a Zimbabwean citizen by birth or descent. It also covers certain persons born outside Zimbabwe to Zimbabwean parents. A child found in Zimbabwe under fifteen whose nationality and parents are unknown is presumed to be a citizen by birth.
contexts: Persons are Zimbabwean citizens by birth if they were born in Zimbabwe and, when they were born, either their mother or father was a Zimbabwean citizen or any of their grandparents was a Zimbabwean citizen by birth or descent. Persons born outside Zimbabwe are Zimbabwean citizens by birth if either of their parents was a Zimbabwean citizen. A child found in Zimbabwe who is, or appears to be, less than fifteen years of age, and whose nationality and parents are not known, is presumed to be a Zimbabwean citizen by birth.

question: What does section 68 provide about administrative justice?
ground_truth: Section 68 provides that every person has a right to administrative conduct that is lawful, prompt, efficient, reasonable, proportionate, impartial, and both substantively and procedurally fair. Anyone adversely affected has the right to prompt written reasons.
contexts: Every person has a right to administrative conduct that is lawful, prompt, efficient, reasonable, proportionate, impartial and both substantively and procedurally fair. Any person whose right, freedom, interest or legitimate expectation has been adversely affected by administrative conduct has the right to be given promptly and in writing the reasons for the conduct.

question: What privacy rights are protected under section 57?
ground_truth: Section 57 protects the right to privacy including protection against entry into one's home, premises or property without permission, searches, seizure of possessions, infringement of private communications, and disclosure of a person's health condition.
contexts: Every person has the right to privacy, which includes the right not to have their home, premises or property entered without their permission, their person, home, premises or property searched, their possessions seized, the privacy of their communications infringed, or their health condition disclosed.
